entries.title: Ania Solo Starts as Low-Status Survivor

content.fact: Ania Solo is a far-future Solo descendant operating at ground level, far removed from galactic power centers.
content.injection: In Legacy II opening scenes, write Ania as a scrappy local survivor, not as a senator, Jedi, or dynastic princess.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.
Respect story-position bounds from Ania Solo Era Opens to Legacy II Resolution.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends far-future Legacy comics era covering Star Wars: Legacy volume 1 with Cade Skywalker, Darth Krayt's One Sith, the Fel Empire, Galactic Alliance Remnant, Jedi survivors, and Legacy volume 2 with Ania Solo and late Sith/Imperial Knight fallout. Excludes Disney canon sequel-era material and earlier post-Endor novel arcs except as historical background or future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Use as a far-future Legends branch. Do not stack as a direct sequel-state for Disney canon or for unresolved custom post-Endor branches unless the story explicitly chooses the Legacy comics trajectory.

content.fact: After Krayt’s defeat, surviving Sith remain dangerous, fragmented, and targeted by intra-Sith violence and Imperial/Jedi pursuit.
content.injection: In Legacy II, do not treat the One Sith as simply gone; use hunted remnants and violent succession fallout. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.

content.injection: When Jao appears, frame him through Imperial Knight discipline and duty, not Jedi informality. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: AG-37 Is a Clone Wars-Era Droid Relic

content.fact: AG-37 is an old combat droid whose long memory and battlefield competence distinguish him from disposable modern hardware.
content.injection: Use AG-37 as a veteran relic with old-war perspective, not as a fresh mass-produced droid. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.

content.injection: Use Sauk to anchor Ania’s story in local danger and personal loyalty rather than galactic dynastic abstraction.

entries.title: Ania Lineage Gives No Instant Authority

content.fact: Ania Solo’s surname has lineage weight but does not give her automatic command, political office, or Jedi status.
content.injection: Do not let Ania’s Solo bloodline automatically make her a leader or Force-trained figure unless the story establishes it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.
Respect story-position bounds from Ania Solo Era Opens to Legacy II Resolution.

content.fact: Jao Assam is eventually enslaved by the dark side, turning his rescue into a personal and ideological crisis.
content.injection: After Jao’s dark-side enslavement begins, write him as compromised and in danger; before that point, do not foreshadow with public certainty.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Jao dark-side enslavement.

entries.title: Ania Fights Beside Empress and Imperial Knights

content.fact: Ania and her allies eventually fight alongside the Empress and Imperial Knights against Sith forces.
content.injection: In late Legacy II, allow Ania’s ground-level crew to intersect with Imperial high politics and Sith war stakes.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy II late alliance.
Respect story-position bounds from Ania and Imperial Knights Fight Beside the Empress to Legacy II Resolution.

entries.title: Cade Not Center Stage in Legacy II

content.fact: Legacy II shifts the primary point of view to Ania Solo; Cade Skywalker should not dominate every Legacy II scene by default.
content.injection: In Ania Solo-era Context, do not over-inject Cade unless directly relevant; Legacy II has its own cast and stakes.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.

entries.title: Legacy II Sith Are Remnant Fallout

content.fact: Legacy II Sith conflict grows out of remnant and succession fallout, not a stable Krayt-led regime.
content.injection: In Legacy II, do not portray Darth Krayt as still openly ruling; use post-Krayt Sith fragmentation unless branch changes it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.

entries.title: Legacy II Blocks Disney Sequel State

content.fact: Legacy II belongs to Legends far-future continuity and should not inherit Disney sequel-trilogy politics, First Order history, or Rey-era Jedi state.
content.injection: When Legacy II is active, block Disney sequel-era assumptions unless an explicit crossover deck overrides them.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Legacy Volume 2 / Ania Solo era.
